description_long: This object is a small bird with a streamlined body, predominantly covered in iridescent green feathers on its back and head, transitioning to darker, almost blackish-brown wings and tail. The bird has a long, slender, needle-like beak, ideal for probing flowers for nectar. Its underparts appear lighter, with a subtle gradient from green to a muted grayish tone. The eyes are small and dark, set slightly forward on the head. The tail is straight and tapers to a point, while the legs are short and not prominently visible. The overall appearance suggests a delicate, agile bird often seen hovering near flowers in urban parks or gardens.
description: A small, iridescent green bird with a long, needle-like beak and dark wings, featuring a streamlined body and pointed tail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's elongated body, green back, and long, straight beak.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tapered tail and the symmetry of the wings folded along the body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the dark wings, green upperparts, and the full length of the beak and tail.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the pointed beak, rounded head, and the narrow profile of the body.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid iridescent green on head and back, with darker solid wings and tail
